name: RABBIT-CAGE-BACKLOG-5
title: Eliminate hardcoded/absolute paths: rabbit-config owns ENV vars, MD files purged of path literals
description:
Two related problems, one coordinated fix:

1. HARDCODED PATHS IN SPECS AND SCRIPTS: Many feature specs and shell/Python scripts reference absolute paths or repo-relative paths as string literals. These break when the workspace root moves or when a user project root differs from the rabbit root.

2. ENV VAR OWNERSHIP IN RABBIT-CONFIG: rabbit-config should define and export canonical ENV vars for all path roots (e.g. RABBIT_ROOT, USER_PROJECT_ROOT, and any feature-specific roots). Scripts source these rather than hardcoding paths.

3. MD FILES MUST NOT USE ENV VARS OR ABSOLUTE PATHS: Claude Code does not expand environment variables in .md files (CLAUDE.md, skill files, spec files, etc.). A thorough audit of all .md files is required to remove any ENV var references (e.g. $RABBIT_ROOT) and absolute path literals. Paths in MD context must be expressed as repo-relative paths or as human-readable descriptions only.

Work breakdown:
- Audit all .md files across .claude/features/ for ENV var usage and absolute paths; rewrite as relative or descriptive.
- Audit all .sh and .py scripts for hardcoded paths; replace with ENV var lookups.
- Add rabbit-config subcommand (e.g. rabbit-config paths) that exports canonical path ENV vars.
- Add contract enforcement check: flag any new MD file that contains an absolute path or $VAR pattern.

Cross-feature impact: contract (enforcement rule), rabbit-cage (rabbit-config command + script audit).

history.note: Deferred: too large/complex for housekeeping waves. Requires dedicated design cycle. Filed for future roadmap consideration.
substantive pre-existing item (filed 2026-05-15); reopening for planning/implementation per user audit 2026-05-18
Temporarily closed before CONTRACT-BACKLOG-19 housekeeping wave to prevent accidental closure by wave b/b filings; will be reopened after the wave completes

Investigation confirms zero hardcoded /home, /Users, or $RABBIT_ROOT references across features; no $VAR usage in MD files; build.py already uses RABBIT_ROOT ENV var. The drift this backlog was filed to prevent never materialized. The remaining proposal (rabbit-config 'paths' subcommand + contract enforcement) is a feature-add, not a fix — file separately if desired. Closing as won't-fix per Designed Deprecation.
